items.name: Msgr. Roger Landry
items.role: National Director, The Pontifical Mission Societies · Member, Vatican Dicastery for Evangelization
items.quote: Christa Dalmazio makes everything she’s involved with beautiful. Whether she’s singing (opera, theater or liturgical music), performing (piano, organ or harp, viola, violin or cello), directing (small ensembles or choirs), or teaching, she brings a passion for excellence and execution that always elevates. She is an artist and person of the highest caliber.

items.name: Dr. Frank La Rocca
items.role: Composer-in-Residence · Benedict XVI Institute for Sacred Music and Liturgy
items.quote: Christa's dedicated work in sacred music—including her work with Higher Word—is vitally important to the restoration of the Beautiful and the healing of our culture.

items.name: Fr. Jonah Teller, O.P.
items.role: Parochial Vicar · Church of St. Joseph, Greenwich Village, New York
items.quote: The joy Christa takes in her work is a delight, and it is so encouraging to know that she is putting her talents at the service of the Church’s worship of God.

items.name: Rev. H. Todd Carter
items.role: Pastor, Holy Innocents Church · Neptune, New Jersey
items.quote: When Christa performs, it is like the angels themselves are visiting the Mass. When she teaches, she’s like a wise nurturing mother who wants you to do your best. She does everything to the best of her ability and always for the greater glory of God.

items.name: Peter Carter
items.role: Founder & Director, Catholic Sacred Music Project · Co-Author (with Cardinal Robert Sarah), The Song of the Lamb
items.quote: Christa's work in sacred music is a great gift to the Church and a clear expression of her love for Our Lord! God has used her to help thousands of people to pray through her music, and she is playing an integral role in the current renewal of sacred music in the Church.

items.name: Spencer McIntosh
items.quote: Christa is not from this world. I’ve never seen someone with the drive, intensity, talent, and hunger that Christa has, and to see it channeled into our faith is remarkable. She took a duet of musicians and helped scale our organization into a major orchestra and choir of 60 Catholics and Christians, filling churches in NYC and essentially inventing a new market category for orchestras.

items.name: Dr. Carly York
items.quote: Christa Dalmazio is such a talented and genuinely wonderful musician to work with! She is very organized and disciplined with scheduling and Liturgical Music planning. Christa served as soprano soloist for a performance of the Vivaldi Gloria with orchestra and community choir that I conducted, and her lovely voice made the performance so spectacular. Most importantly Christa was a brilliant musical collaborator for my 2025 professionally released EP Album Glorious Mystery, which featured her as the organ and harp accompanist on six Marian Gregorian and sacred chant pieces. Christa is so knowledgeable, and has such a strong, intuitive sense of traditional Catholic music, that her input and suggestions on how to interpret and perform the six songs made the album the beautiful and spiritually inspiring work that it is. I cannot recommend Christa Dalmazio more highly! I look forward to working with her again in the future!
